description_long: This is a single-story rectangular house with a prominent red-tiled gable roof, fully covered by large black solar panels arranged in neat rows. The front facade features a centered brown door flanked by two sets of rectangular windows with black grilles, each window divided into multiple panes. The side walls each have a single window, matching the style of those on the front. The exterior walls are finished in white, providing a clean contrast to the dark roof and window frames. The roof overhangs slightly, creating a subtle eave around the perimeter. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on energy efficiency and modern utility.
description: A single-story white house with a red-tiled gable roof fully covered in black solar panels, featuring a central brown door and multiple rectangular windows with black grilles.
description_view_0: The gable end of the house is visible, showing the red-tiled roof with solar panels and a single window on the side wall.
description_view_1: The front of the house displays the main entry door centered between two sets of rectangular windows, with the full expanse of the solar-panel-covered roof above.
description_view_2: The opposite gable end is shown, highlighting the roof's solar panels and a single window on the side wall.
description_view_3: The rear of the house is visible, showing a plain wall beneath the solar-panel-covered roof with no visible doors or windows.
attribute_entryway_design: Centered single brown door with no porch or steps, flanked by symmetrical windows and minimal decorative elements.
